name: Download Payload Using Edge Headless Feature
notes:
This rule detects downloading of payloads using the Edge headless feature.

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
SIGMA Title: Potential Arbitrary File Download Via MSEdge.EXE
SIGMA Description: Detects usage of the "msedge.exe" binary as a LOLBIN to download arbitrary file via the CLI


Confidence: LOW

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
when the event matches Process Name (custom) is any of msedge.exe
when the event matches Parent Process Name (custom) is any of [cmd.exe or powershell.exe or pwsh.exe or powershell_ise.exe]
when the event matches (LOWER("Command") LIKE '%http://%' OR LOWER("Command") LIKE '%https://%' OR LOWER("Command") LIKE '%ftp://%' OR LOWER("Command") LIKE '%www.%') AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Download Payload Using Edge Headless Feature on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
AND when the event matches Process Name (custom) is any of msedge.exe
AND when the event matches Parent Process Name (custom) is any of [cmd.exe or powershell.exe or pwsh.exe or powershell_ise.exe]
AND when the event matches (LOWER("Command") LIKE '%http://%' OR LOWER("Command") LIKE '%https://%' OR LOWER("Command") LIKE '%ftp://%' OR LOWER("Command") LIKE '%www.%') AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: Download Payload Using Edge Headless Feature
Event description: Detects downloading of payloads using the Edge headless feature.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
